title: Postgres 19 Compression: from pglz to LZ4 | Crunchy Data Blog
excerpt: With Postgres 19 switching the default from pglz to LZ4, this post walks through the compression decision path, storage strategies, and index-size limits that shape real-world behavior.
content_html:
Postgres 19 is planning to change the default TOAST compression from pglz to LZ4, so let's look at how Postgres compresses data in table storage and indexes. Postgres uses a single, unified compression framework for table (heap), TOAST, and indexes. In heap and TOAST, compression is automatic and on by default for variable-length types like TEXT, VARCHAR, BYTEA, and JSONB. In indexes, it is opportunistic: compression fires only when an individual key exceeds the size threshold, not for every variable-length value stored in an index.

click to expand

History of Postgres Compression

Compression was first added in Postgres 7.0, released in 2000, but not in the form it takes today. At the time, Postgres had a strict 8kB maximum row size, and trying to insert more than 8kB would throw an error. Fixing this row size limit was a priority for the Postgres core team.

The first attempt to work around the limit was an explicitly compressed field. Postgres 7.0 shipped an lztext data type that used the pglz compression algorithm. This implementation had tradeoffs: the 8kB row limit still existed, and users had to explicitly choose a compressed data type.

After the 7.0 release, the next logical step might have been to add more compressed data types like LONG or BLOB (as many closed source databases were doing at the time). Instead, the Postgres core team rejected additional data types and rallied around TOAST. Discussions on the pgsql-hackers mailing list show the group split the 8kB problem into two distinct problems: a data type problem and a physical storage problem. Compression and TOAST were the answer to the physical storage problem.

TOAST with pglz

With Postgres 7.1, TOAST was implemented using pglz.

The pglz algorithm lives in the pg_lzcompress.c file. Because Postgres is open source, we can read the author's reasoning for home-rolling a compression algorithm right in the comments:

  • Trade ratio for speed: pglz is fast to compress and decompress, and willingly gives up compression ratio to get there. The source code calls this another "speed against ratio" preference characteristic of the algorithm.
  • Minimal memory usage: the algorithm uses a 4096-byte sliding window, small enough not to affect Postgres' memory needs. The comments note that the compressor works best for attributes of a size between 1K and 1M, so it trades away performance on very large values.
  • Aggressive fail-safe: the algorithm is designed to fail fast when data does not compress well, to avoid wasting CPU cycles.
  • No external dependencies: the algorithm is self-contained and does not require any external libraries. Back then, Linux was not the default server OS for the cloud (nor was there a "cloud"), so Postgres needed a compression algorithm that would work everywhere it was compiled.

Jan Wieck authored the algorithm and left an acknowledgement at the bottom of the file:

Many thanks to Adisak Pochanayon, who's article about SLZ inspired me to write the PostgreSQL compression this way.

Adisak Pochanayon's SLZ article described a compression scheme built for the game industry, where it was used to compress graphics and audio data.

Why move from pglz to LZ4?

pglz was built for a different era, and LZ4 is a modern algorithm whose tradeoffs match modern hardware. Postgres' LZ4 rollout follows a path similar to the original pglz implementation: first as an option, then as standard. Since Postgres 14, LZ4 has been available via a system-wide setting (default_toast_compression = 'lz4') or a column-specific setting (column_name text COMPRESSION lz4).

  • Faster compression: LZ4 compresses significantly faster than pglz. On an totally unscientific test, a 2,000 row INSERT of ~10 kB values, LZ4 completes in roughly 6 ms versus 50 ms for pglz (8× faster). Decompression throughput is similar between the two algorithms with this data set, as I/O and memory latency dominated over CPU decode time.
  • Better compression ratio (sometimes): LZ4's 64 kB sliding window (versus pglz's 4 kB) finds more back-references in the data, which expands compression. On a similar, totally unscientific workload, LZ4 produced 111 bytes stored per 10,400 bytes raw (98.9% reduction) versus pglz's 186 bytes (98.2% reduction), and used 312 kB of total heap space versus pglz's 472 kB. There are situations where pglz has a better compression ratio.
  • Continues to fail fast: LZ4 has an efficient early-abort mechanism designed to detect random or incompressible data quickly.

Postgres storage strategies

First off, you need to know that Postgres has a few different storage strategies for columns:

EXTENDED (we capitalize it because the Postgres docs do, not because we're yelling) lets Postgres use every tool available. This is the default for variable-length types like TEXT, VARCHAR, BYTEA, and JSONB. Values can be stored uncompressed, compressed, in the heap, or in TOAST.

PLAIN stores the column inline in the heap, uncompressed. It is the default for fixed-width types like INT, FLOAT, and BOOL. These values rarely benefit from compression.

EXTERNAL tells Postgres to store the column in TOAST, but not compress it.

MAIN tells Postgres to try compressing the column, but avoid moving it to TOAST if possible.

The varlena format

For variable-length types, Postgres uses a format called varlena to store the data. varlena is a self-describing format with a header that records the length of the data and whether it is compressed. It is used for all variable-length types (including TEXT, VARCHAR, BYTEA, and JSONB) and it is what gets stored in the heap, in TOAST, and in indexes.

Only variable-length types are compressed. Fixed-length types (like INT, FLOAT, and BOOL) are never compressed; they are stored directly in the heap.

The compression decision tree

When writing data (insert or update), Postgres attempts to get the row size below a threshold of roughly 2 kB (toast_tuple_target, default 2040 bytes). It uses the following decision tree for EXTENDED columns:

  1. If the entire row is already smaller than ~2 kB, Postgres writes the row directly to the heap, uncompressed.
  2. If the row exceeds the threshold, Postgres sorts the EXTENDED columns by size and attempts to compress the largest one. If compression succeeds and brings the total row size under the threshold, it stops and writes to the heap. If not, it moves to the next largest column.
  3. If all eligible columns are compressed and the row still exceeds the threshold, Postgres begins moving the largest EXTENDED or EXTERNAL columns out-of-line into the TOAST table, replacing each with an 18-byte pointer in the main heap tuple until the row fits.
  4. If it still doesn't fit, Postgres loops back to compress any MAIN columns inline. If that fails, it takes the last resort: moving those MAIN columns out-of-line into TOAST.

For more on TOAST, check out Postgres TOAST: The Greatest Thing Since Sliced Bread?

Checking actual compression savings

-- pg_column_size: bytes as stored in the heap or TOAST table (after compression)
-- octet_length:  bytes of raw character data (uncompressed)

SELECT
  pg_column_size(payload) AS stored_bytes,
  octet_length(payload)   AS raw_bytes,
  round(
    (1 - pg_column_size(payload)::numeric
          / NULLIF(octet_length(payload), 0)) * 100, 1
  ) AS compression_pct
FROM events WHERE length(payload) > 100 LIMIT 5;

pg_column_size reports the compressed data size. When it returns a value much smaller than octet_length, the value was successfully compressed. When the two are approximately equal, the value did not compress well enough to save space. In that case, if the value is large (above the ~2 kB threshold), it will still have been moved to the TOAST table uncompressed.

Compression in indexes

B-tree index pages store key values as IndexTuple entries. Each entry contains an IndexTupleData header (8 bytes) followed by the key datum. For variable-length types, the datum uses the same varlena format as heap tuples (piggybacking on the workaround built for the 8kB row limit).

Postgres uses a single compression framework tied to the TOAST architecture. It flags in the varlena header whether the data is compressed. If the heap already compressed a value, it goes into the index compressed. If a value is uncompressed and exceeds 510 bytes (TOAST_INDEX_TARGET, about 1/16 of the 8 kB buffer page), the index code invokes the same TOAST compression routine inline to try to make it fit. If the compressed form fits, it is stored compressed. If even the compressed form exceeds the limit, the write transaction fails.

This is why repeat('x', 5000) can be B-tree indexed: LZ4 compresses 5,000 repeated characters down to ~38 bytes, well within the 2704-byte cap. Random or pseudo-random data of the same length produces a compressed form nearly equal to the original, which exceeds the cap and cannot be indexed.

-- These succeed: both compressible, both fit after LZ4 compression
CREATE INDEX ON docs (body);

INSERT INTO docs VALUES (repeat('x', 5000));    -- stored: ~38 bytes compressed
INSERT INTO docs VALUES (repeat('ab', 2000));   -- stored: ~35 bytes compressed

-- This fails: md5 output is pseudo-random, essentially incompressible
INSERT INTO docs VALUES (
  (SELECT string_agg(md5(g::text), '') FROM generate_series(1, 88) g)
);
-- 2816 chars of MD5 → compressed form ≈ 2816 bytes → index row size 2832 > 2704
-- ERROR:  index row size 2832 exceeds btree version 4 maximum 2704 for index "..."
-- HINT:   Values larger than 1/3 of a buffer page cannot be indexed.

The future of compression in Postgres

There's a lot to learn about how Postgres moves forward by looking at compression. The early false step of dedicated compressed data types was acknowledged, and the underlying pglz work was retooled into TOAST, which has been a huge success. In moving from pglz to LZ4, Postgres is taking a similar approach: first a test, then a migration. The core team has moved intentionally to make sure the compression algorithm change is the correct path.

blocks.html_en: Postgres 19 is planning to change the default TOAST compression from pglz to LZ4, so let's look at how Postgres compresses data in table storage and indexes. Postgres uses a single, unified compression framework for table (heap), TOAST, and indexes. In heap and TOAST, compression is automatic and on by default for variable-length types like TEXT, VARCHAR, BYTEA, and JSONB. In indexes, it is opportunistic: compression fires only when an individual key exceeds the size threshold, not for every variable-length value stored in an index.

sentences.en: Postgres 19 is planning to change the default TOAST compression from pglz to LZ4, so let's look at how Postgres compresses data in table storage and indexes.
sentences.zh: Postgres 19 计划将默认的 TOAST 压缩从 pglz 更改为 LZ4，所以让我们看看 Postgres 如何压缩表存储和索引中的数据。

sentences.en: Postgres uses a single, unified compression framework for table (heap), TOAST, and indexes.
sentences.zh: Postgres 对表（堆）、TOAST 和索引使用单一、统一的压缩框架。

sentences.en: In heap and TOAST, compression is automatic and on by default for variable-length types like TEXT, VARCHAR, BYTEA, and JSONB.
sentences.zh: 在堆和 TOAST 中，压缩是自动的，并且对于 TEXT、VARCHAR、BYTEA 和 JSONB 等可变长度类型默认启用。

sentences.en: In indexes, it is opportunistic: compression fires only when an individual key exceeds the size threshold, not for every variable-length value stored in an index.
sentences.zh: 在索引中，它是机会主义的：仅当单个键超过大小阈值时才会触发压缩，而不是针对存储在索引中的每个可变长度值。

sentences.zh: 相反，Postgres 核心团队拒绝了额外的数据类型并团结在 TOAST 周围。

sentences.en: Compression and TOAST were the answer to the physical storage problem.
sentences.zh: 压缩和 TOAST 是物理存储问题的答案。

blocks.html_en: • Trade ratio for speed: pglz is fast to compress and decompress, and willingly gives up compression ratio to get there. The source code calls this another "speed against ratio" preference characteristic of the algorithm.

sentences.zh: 以速度换取速度：pglz 压缩和解压缩速度很快，并且愿意放弃压缩比来达到目的。

sentences.en: The source code calls this another "speed against ratio" preference characteristic of the algorithm.

sentences.en: Minimal memory usage: the algorithm uses a 4096-byte sliding window, small enough not to affect Postgres' memory needs.
sentences.zh: 最小内存使用：该算法使用 4096 字节的滑动窗口，足够小，不会影响 Postgres 的内存需求。

sentences.en: The comments note that the compressor works best for attributes of a size between 1K and 1M, so it trades away performance on very large values.
sentences.zh: 评论指出，压缩器最适合大小在 1K 到 1M 之间的属性，因此它会牺牲非常大的值的性能。

blocks.html_en: • Aggressive fail-safe: the algorithm is designed to fail fast when data does not compress well, to avoid wasting CPU cycles.

sentences.en: Aggressive fail-safe: the algorithm is designed to fail fast when data does not compress well, to avoid wasting CPU cycles.
sentences.zh: 积极的故障安全：该算法旨在在数据压缩不佳时快速失败，以避免浪费 CPU 周期。

blocks.html_en: • No external dependencies: the algorithm is self-contained and does not require any external libraries. Back then, Linux was not the default server OS for the cloud (nor was there a "cloud"), so Postgres needed a compression algorithm that would work everywhere it was compiled.

sentences.en: No external dependencies: the algorithm is self-contained and does not require any external libraries.
sentences.zh: 无外部依赖：算法是独立的，不需要任何外部库。

sentences.en: Back then, Linux was not the default server OS for the cloud (nor was there a "cloud"), so Postgres needed a compression algorithm that would work everywhere it was compiled.

sentences.en: Many thanks to Adisak Pochanayon, who's article about SLZ inspired me to write the PostgreSQL compression this way.
sentences.zh: 非常感谢 Adisak Pochanayon，他关于 SLZ 的文章启发我以这种方式编写 PostgreSQL 压缩。

blocks.html_en: Adisak Pochanayon's SLZ article described a compression scheme built for the game industry, where it was used to compress graphics and audio data.

sentences.en: Adisak Pochanayon's SLZ article described a compression scheme built for the game industry, where it was used to compress graphics and audio data.
sentences.zh: Adisak Pochanayon 的 SLZ 文章描述了一种为游戏行业构建的压缩方案，用于压缩图形和音频数据。

blocks.html_en: Why move from pglz to LZ4?

sentences.en: Why move from pglz to LZ4?
sentences.zh: 为什么从pglz转到LZ4？

blocks.html_en: pglz was built for a different era, and LZ4 is a modern algorithm whose tradeoffs match modern hardware. Postgres' LZ4 rollout follows a path similar to the original pglz implementation: first as an option, then as standard. Since Postgres 14, LZ4 has been available via a system-wide setting (default_toast_compression = 'lz4') or a column-specific setting (column_name text COMPRESSION lz4).

sentences.en: pglz was built for a different era, and LZ4 is a modern algorithm whose tradeoffs match modern hardware.
sentences.zh: pglz 是为不同的时代而构建的，而 LZ4 是一种现代算法，其权衡与现代硬件相匹配。

sentences.en: Postgres' LZ4 rollout follows a path similar to the original pglz implementation: first as an option, then as standard.
sentences.zh: Postgres 的 LZ4 推出遵循与原始 pglz 实现类似的路径：首先作为选项，然后作为标准。

sentences.en: Since Postgres 14, LZ4 has been available via a system-wide setting (default_toast_compression = 'lz4') or a column-specific setting (column_name text COMPRESSION lz4).
sentences.zh: 从 Postgres 14 开始，LZ4 可以通过系统范围的设置 (default_toast_compression = 'lz4') 或特定于列的设置 (column_name text COMPRESSION lz4) 来使用。

blocks.html_en: • Faster compression: LZ4 compresses significantly faster than pglz. On an totally unscientific test, a 2,000 row INSERT of ~10 kB values, LZ4 completes in roughly 6 ms versus 50 ms for pglz (8× faster). Decompression throughput is similar between the two algorithms with this data set, as I/O and memory latency dominated over CPU decode time.

sentences.en: Faster compression: LZ4 compresses significantly faster than pglz.
sentences.zh: 更快的压缩：LZ4 的压缩速度明显快于 pglz。

sentences.en: On an totally unscientific test, a 2,000 row INSERT of ~10 kB values, LZ4 completes in roughly 6 ms versus 50 ms for pglz (8× faster).
sentences.zh: 在一项完全不科学的测试中，大约 10 kB 值的 2,000 行插入，LZ4 在大约 6 毫秒内完成，而 pglz 则为 50 毫秒（快 8 倍）。

sentences.en: Decompression throughput is similar between the two algorithms with this data set, as I/O and memory latency dominated over CPU decode time.
sentences.zh: 对于该数据集，两种算法的解压吞吐量相似，因为 I/O 和内存延迟主导了 CPU 解码时间。

blocks.html_en: • Better compression ratio (sometimes): LZ4's 64 kB sliding window (versus pglz's 4 kB) finds more back-references in the data, which expands compression. On a similar, totally unscientific workload, LZ4 produced 111 bytes stored per 10,400 bytes raw (98.9% reduction) versus pglz's 186 bytes (98.2% reduction), and used 312 kB of total heap space versus pglz's 472 kB. There are situations where pglz has a better compression ratio.

sentences.en: Better compression ratio (sometimes): LZ4's 64 kB sliding window (versus pglz's 4 kB) finds more back-references in the data, which expands compression.
sentences.zh: 更好的压缩比（有时）：LZ4 的 64 kB 滑动窗口（相对于 pglz 的 4 kB）可以在数据中找到更多反向引用，从而扩大压缩范围。

sentences.en: On a similar, totally unscientific workload, LZ4 produced 111 bytes stored per 10,400 bytes raw (98.9% reduction) versus pglz's 186 bytes (98.2% reduction), and used 312 kB of total heap space versus pglz's 472 kB.
sentences.zh: 在类似的、完全不科学的工作负载中，LZ4 每 10,400 字节原始存储 111 字节（减少 98.9%），而 pglz 为 186 字节（减少 98.2%），并且使用了 312 kB 的总堆空间，而 pglz 为 472 kB。

sentences.en: There are situations where pglz has a better compression ratio.
sentences.zh: 在某些情况下，pglz 具有更好的压缩比。

blocks.html_en: • Continues to fail fast: LZ4 has an efficient early-abort mechanism designed to detect random or incompressible data quickly.

sentences.en: Continues to fail fast: LZ4 has an efficient early-abort mechanism designed to detect random or incompressible data quickly.
sentences.zh: 持续快速失败：LZ4 具有高效的早期中止机制，旨在快速检测随机或不可压缩数据。

blocks.html_en: Postgres storage strategies

sentences.en: Postgres storage strategies
sentences.zh: Postgres 存储策略

blocks.html_en: First off, you need to know that Postgres has a few different storage strategies for columns:

sentences.en: First off, you need to know that Postgres has a few different storage strategies for columns:
sentences.zh: 首先，您需要知道 Postgres 对列有几种不同的存储策略：

blocks.html_en: EXTENDED (we capitalize it because the Postgres docs do, not because we're yelling) lets Postgres use every tool available. This is the default for variable-length types like TEXT, VARCHAR, BYTEA, and JSONB. Values can be stored uncompressed, compressed, in the heap, or in TOAST.

sentences.en: EXTENDED (we capitalize it because the Postgres docs do, not because we're yelling) lets Postgres use every tool available.
sentences.zh: EXTENDED（我们将其大写是因为 Postgres 文档这样做，而不是因为我们大喊大叫）让 Postgres 使用所有可用的工具。

sentences.en: This is the default for variable-length types like TEXT, VARCHAR, BYTEA, and JSONB.
sentences.zh: 这是 TEXT、VARCHAR、BYTEA 和 JSONB 等可变长度类型的默认设置。

sentences.en: Values can be stored uncompressed, compressed, in the heap, or in TOAST.
sentences.zh: 值可以以未压缩、压缩的方式存储在堆中或 TOAST 中。

blocks.html_en: PLAIN stores the column inline in the heap, uncompressed. It is the default for fixed-width types like INT, FLOAT, and BOOL. These values rarely benefit from compression.

sentences.en: PLAIN stores the column inline in the heap, uncompressed.
sentences.zh: PLAIN 将列内联存储在堆中，未压缩。

sentences.en: It is the default for fixed-width types like INT, FLOAT, and BOOL.
sentences.zh: 它是 INT、FLOAT 和 BOOL 等固定宽度类型的默认值。

sentences.en: These values rarely benefit from compression.
sentences.zh: 这些值很少从压缩中受益。

blocks.html_en: EXTERNAL tells Postgres to store the column in TOAST, but not compress it.

sentences.en: EXTERNAL tells Postgres to store the column in TOAST, but not compress it.
sentences.zh: EXTERNAL 告诉 Postgres 将列存储在 TOAST 中，但不压缩它。

blocks.html_en: MAIN tells Postgres to try compressing the column, but avoid moving it to TOAST if possible.

sentences.en: MAIN tells Postgres to try compressing the column, but avoid moving it to TOAST if possible.
sentences.zh: MAIN 告诉 Postgres 尝试压缩列，但如果可能的话避免将其移动到 TOAST。

blocks.html_en: The varlena format

sentences.en: The varlena format
sentences.zh: 瓦莱纳格式

blocks.html_en: For variable-length types, Postgres uses a format called varlena to store the data. varlena is a self-describing format with a header that records the length of the data and whether it is compressed. It is used for all variable-length types (including TEXT, VARCHAR, BYTEA, and JSONB) and it is what gets stored in the heap, in TOAST, and in indexes.

sentences.en: For variable-length types, Postgres uses a format called varlena to store the data.
sentences.zh: 对于可变长度类型，Postgres 使用一种称为 varlena 的格式来存储数据。

sentences.en: varlena is a self-describing format with a header that records the length of the data and whether it is compressed.
sentences.zh: varlena 是一种自描述格式，其标头记录了数据的长度以及是否被压缩。

sentences.en: It is used for all variable-length types (including TEXT, VARCHAR, BYTEA, and JSONB) and it is what gets stored in the heap, in TOAST, and in indexes.
sentences.zh: 它用于所有可变长度类型（包括 TEXT、VARCHAR、BYTEA 和 JSONB），并且存储在堆、TOAST 和索引中。

blocks.html_en: Only variable-length types are compressed. Fixed-length types (like INT, FLOAT, and BOOL) are never compressed; they are stored directly in the heap.

sentences.en: Only variable-length types are compressed.
sentences.zh: 仅压缩可变长度类型。

sentences.en: Fixed-length types (like INT, FLOAT, and BOOL) are never compressed; they are stored directly in the heap.
sentences.zh: 固定长度类型（如 INT、FLOAT 和 BOOL）永远不会被压缩；它们直接存储在堆中。

blocks.html_en: The compression decision tree

sentences.en: The compression decision tree
sentences.zh: 压缩决策树

blocks.html_en: When writing data (insert or update), Postgres attempts to get the row size below a threshold of roughly 2 kB (toast_tuple_target, default 2040 bytes). It uses the following decision tree for EXTENDED columns:

sentences.en: When writing data (insert or update), Postgres attempts to get the row size below a threshold of roughly 2 kB (toast_tuple_target, default 2040 bytes).
sentences.zh: 当写入数据（插入或更新）时，Postgres 尝试使行大小低于大约 2 kB 的阈值（toast_tuple_target，默认 2040 字节）。

sentences.en: It uses the following decision tree for EXTENDED columns:
sentences.zh: 它对扩展列使用以下决策树：

blocks.html_en: • If the entire row is already smaller than ~2 kB, Postgres writes the row directly to the heap, uncompressed.

sentences.en: If the entire row is already smaller than ~2 kB, Postgres writes the row directly to the heap, uncompressed.
sentences.zh: 如果整行已经小于约 2 kB，Postgres 会将该行直接写入堆，不进行压缩。

blocks.html_en: • If the row exceeds the threshold, Postgres sorts the EXTENDED columns by size and attempts to compress the largest one. If compression succeeds and brings the total row size under the threshold, it stops and writes to the heap. If not, it moves to the next largest column.

sentences.en: If the row exceeds the threshold, Postgres sorts the EXTENDED columns by size and attempts to compress the largest one.
sentences.zh: 如果该行超过阈值，Postgres 将按大小对 EXTENDED 列进行排序，并尝试压缩最大的列。

sentences.en: If compression succeeds and brings the total row size under the threshold, it stops and writes to the heap.
sentences.zh: 如果压缩成功并使总行大小低于阈值，它将停止并写入堆。

sentences.en: If not, it moves to the next largest column.
sentences.zh: 如果不是，它将移动到下一个最大的列。

blocks.html_en: • If all eligible columns are compressed and the row still exceeds the threshold, Postgres begins moving the largest EXTENDED or EXTERNAL columns out-of-line into the TOAST table, replacing each with an 18-byte pointer in the main heap tuple until the row fits.

sentences.en: If all eligible columns are compressed and the row still exceeds the threshold, Postgres begins moving the largest EXTENDED or EXTERNAL columns out-of-line into the TOAST table, replacing each with an 18-byte pointer in the main heap tuple until the row fits.
sentences.zh: 如果所有符合条件的列都被压缩并且该行仍然超过阈值，Postgres 开始将最大的 EXTENDED 或 EXTERNAL 列移出行外到 TOAST 表中，并用主堆元组中的 18 字节指针替换每个列，直到该行适合为止。

blocks.html_en: • If it still doesn't fit, Postgres loops back to compress any MAIN columns inline. If that fails, it takes the last resort: moving those MAIN columns out-of-line into TOAST.

sentences.en: If it still doesn't fit, Postgres loops back to compress any MAIN columns inline.
sentences.zh: 如果仍然不适合，Postgres 会循环返回以内联压缩所有 MAIN 列。

sentences.en: If that fails, it takes the last resort: moving those MAIN columns out-of-line into TOAST.
sentences.zh: 如果失败，则采取最后的手段：将那些 MAIN 列移至 TOAST 中。

blocks.html_en: For more on TOAST, check out Postgres TOAST: The Greatest Thing Since Sliced Bread?

sentences.en: For more on TOAST, check out Postgres TOAST: The Greatest Thing Since Sliced Bread?
sentences.zh: 有关 TOAST 的更多信息，请查看 Postgres TOAST：自切片面包以来最伟大的事情？

blocks.html_en: Checking actual compression savings

sentences.en: Checking actual compression savings
sentences.zh: 检查实际压缩节省

blocks.html_en: pg_column_size reports the compressed data size. When it returns a value much smaller than octet_length, the value was successfully compressed. When the two are approximately equal, the value did not compress well enough to save space. In that case, if the value is large (above the ~2 kB threshold), it will still have been moved to the TOAST table uncompressed.

sentences.en: pg_column_size reports the compressed data size.
sentences.zh: pg_column_size 报告压缩数据大小。

sentences.en: When it returns a value much smaller than octet_length, the value was successfully compressed.
sentences.zh: 当它返回一个远小于octet_length的值时，该值已成功压缩。

sentences.en: When the two are approximately equal, the value did not compress well enough to save space.
sentences.zh: 当两者大致相等时，该值压缩得不够好，无法节省空间。

sentences.en: In that case, if the value is large (above the ~2 kB threshold), it will still have been moved to the TOAST table uncompressed.
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，如果该值很大（高于 ~2 kB 阈值），它仍会以未压缩的方式移至 TOAST 表。

blocks.html_en: Compression in indexes

sentences.en: Compression in indexes
sentences.zh: 索引压缩

blocks.html_en: B-tree index pages store key values as IndexTuple entries. Each entry contains an IndexTupleData header (8 bytes) followed by the key datum. For variable-length types, the datum uses the same varlena format as heap tuples (piggybacking on the workaround built for the 8kB row limit).

sentences.en: B-tree index pages store key values as IndexTuple entries.
sentences.zh: B 树索引页将键值存储为 IndexTuple 条目。

sentences.en: Each entry contains an IndexTupleData header (8 bytes) followed by the key datum.
sentences.zh: 每个条目包含一个 IndexTupleData 标头（8 个字节），后跟关键数据。

sentences.en: For variable-length types, the datum uses the same varlena format as heap tuples (piggybacking on the workaround built for the 8kB row limit).
sentences.zh: 对于可变长度类型，数据使用与堆元组相同的 varlena 格式（搭载为 8kB 行限制构建的解决方法）。

blocks.html_en: Postgres uses a single compression framework tied to the TOAST architecture. It flags in the varlena header whether the data is compressed. If the heap already compressed a value, it goes into the index compressed. If a value is uncompressed and exceeds 510 bytes (TOAST_INDEX_TARGET, about 1/16 of the 8 kB buffer page), the index code invokes the same TOAST compression routine inline to try to make it fit. If the compressed form fits, it is stored compressed. If even the compressed form exceeds the limit, the write transaction fails.

sentences.en: Postgres uses a single compression framework tied to the TOAST architecture.
sentences.zh: Postgres 使用与 TOAST 架构相关的单一压缩框架。

sentences.en: It flags in the varlena header whether the data is compressed.
sentences.zh: 它在 varlena 标头中标记数据是否被压缩。

sentences.en: If the heap already compressed a value, it goes into the index compressed.
sentences.zh: 如果堆已经压缩了一个值，它将进入压缩索引。

sentences.en: If a value is uncompressed and exceeds 510 bytes (TOAST_INDEX_TARGET, about 1/16 of the 8 kB buffer page), the index code invokes the same TOAST compression routine inline to try to make it fit.
sentences.zh: 如果某个值未压缩且超过 510 字节（TOAST_INDEX_TARGET，大约 8 kB 缓冲区页的 1/16），则索引代码会内联调用相同的 TOAST 压缩例程以尝试使其适合。

sentences.en: If the compressed form fits, it is stored compressed.
sentences.zh: 如果压缩的形式适合，则将其压缩存储。

sentences.en: If even the compressed form exceeds the limit, the write transaction fails.
sentences.zh: 如果压缩形式超过限制，写入事务就会失败。

blocks.html_en: This is why repeat('x', 5000) can be B-tree indexed: LZ4 compresses 5,000 repeated characters down to ~38 bytes, well within the 2704-byte cap. Random or pseudo-random data of the same length produces a compressed form nearly equal to the original, which exceeds the cap and cannot be indexed.

sentences.en: This is why repeat('x', 5000) can be B-tree indexed: LZ4 compresses 5,000 repeated characters down to ~38 bytes, well within the 2704-byte cap.
sentences.zh: 这就是 Repeat('x', 5000) 可以被 B 树索引的原因：LZ4 将 5,000 个重复字符压缩到约 38 个字节，完全在 2704 字节的上限之内。

sentences.en: Random or pseudo-random data of the same length produces a compressed form nearly equal to the original, which exceeds the cap and cannot be indexed.
sentences.zh: 相同长度的随机或伪随机数据产生的压缩形式几乎等于原始数据，超出了上限并且无法索引。

blocks.html_en: The future of compression in Postgres

sentences.en: The future of compression in Postgres
sentences.zh: Postgres 压缩的未来

blocks.html_en: There's a lot to learn about how Postgres moves forward by looking at compression. The early false step of dedicated compressed data types was acknowledged, and the underlying pglz work was retooled into TOAST, which has been a huge success. In moving from pglz to LZ4, Postgres is taking a similar approach: first a test, then a migration. The core team has moved intentionally to make sure the compression algorithm change is the correct path.

sentences.en: There's a lot to learn about how Postgres moves forward by looking at compression.
sentences.zh: 关于 Postgres 如何通过压缩来向前发展，有很多东西需要了解。

sentences.en: The early false step of dedicated compressed data types was acknowledged, and the underlying pglz work was retooled into TOAST, which has been a huge success.
sentences.zh: 专用压缩数据类型的早期错误步骤得到了承认，并且底层 pglz 工作被重新整合到 TOAST 中，这取得了巨大的成功。

sentences.en: In moving from pglz to LZ4, Postgres is taking a similar approach: first a test, then a migration.
sentences.zh: 在从 pglz 迁移到 LZ4 时，Postgres 采取了类似的方法：首先进行测试，然后进行迁移。

sentences.en: The core team has moved intentionally to make sure the compression algorithm change is the correct path.
sentences.zh: 核心团队有意采取行动，以确保压缩算法的更改是正确的路径。

content_html_zh:
Postgres 19 计划将默认的 TOAST 压缩从 pglz 更改为 LZ4，所以让我们看看 Postgres 如何压缩表存储和索引中的数据。 Postgres 对表（堆）、TOAST 和索引使用单一、统一的压缩框架。 在堆和 TOAST 中，压缩是自动的，并且对于 TEXT、VARCHAR、BYTEA 和 JSONB 等可变长度类型默认启用。 在索引中，它是机会主义的：仅当单个键超过大小阈值时才会触发压缩，而不是针对存储在索引中的每个可变长度值。

点击展开

Postgres 压缩的历史

压缩最初是在 2000 年发布的 Postgres 7.0 中添加的，但不是今天的形式。 当时，Postgres 有严格的 8kB 最大行大小，尝试插入超过 8kB 会引发错误。 修复此行大小限制是 Postgres 核心团队的首要任务。

解决该限制的第一次尝试是显式压缩字段。 Postgres 7.0 提供了使用 pglz 压缩算法的 lztext 数据类型。 此实现需要权衡：8kB 行限制仍然存在，并且用户必须显式选择压缩数据类型。

在 7.0 版本之后，下一个逻辑步骤可能是添加更多压缩数据类型，例如 LONG 或 BLOB（正如当时许多闭源数据库所做的那样）。 相反，Postgres 核心团队拒绝了额外的数据类型并团结在 TOAST 周围。 pgsql-hackers 邮件列表上的讨论显示，该小组将 8kB 问题分为两个不同的问题：数据类型问题和物理存储问题。 压缩和 TOAST 是物理存储问题的答案。

与 pglz 一起吐司

在 Postgres 7.1 中，TOAST 是使用 pglz 实现的。

pglz 算法位于 pg_lzcompress.c 文件中。 由于 Postgres 是开源的，我们可以在评论中阅读作者自行推出压缩算法的推理：

  • 以速度换取速度：pglz 压缩和解压缩速度很快，并且愿意放弃压缩比来达到目的。 源代码将此称为算法的另一个“速度与比率”偏好特征。
  • 最小内存使用：该算法使用 4096 字节的滑动窗口，足够小，不会影响 Postgres 的内存需求。 评论指出，压缩器最适合大小在 1K 到 1M 之间的属性，因此它会牺牲非常大的值的性能。
  • 积极的故障安全：该算法旨在在数据压缩不佳时快速失败，以避免浪费 CPU 周期。
  • 无外部依赖：算法是独立的，不需要任何外部库。 当时，Linux 还不是云的默认服务器操作系统（也不存在“云”），因此 Postgres 需要一种可以在编译的任何地方工作的压缩算法。

Jan Wieck 创作了该算法，并在文件底部留下了致谢：

非常感谢 Adisak Pochanayon，他关于 SLZ 的文章启发我以这种方式编写 PostgreSQL 压缩。

Adisak Pochanayon 的 SLZ 文章描述了一种为游戏行业构建的压缩方案，用于压缩图形和音频数据。

为什么从pglz转到LZ4？

pglz 是为不同的时代而构建的，而 LZ4 是一种现代算法，其权衡与现代硬件相匹配。 Postgres 的 LZ4 推出遵循与原始 pglz 实现类似的路径：首先作为选项，然后作为标准。 从 Postgres 14 开始，LZ4 可以通过系统范围的设置 (default_toast_compression = 'lz4') 或特定于列的设置 (column_name text COMPRESSION lz4) 来使用。

  • 更快的压缩：LZ4 的压缩速度明显快于 pglz。 在一项完全不科学的测试中，大约 10 kB 值的 2,000 行插入，LZ4 在大约 6 毫秒内完成，而 pglz 则为 50 毫秒（快 8 倍）。 对于该数据集，两种算法的解压吞吐量相似，因为 I/O 和内存延迟主导了 CPU 解码时间。
  • 更好的压缩比（有时）：LZ4 的 64 kB 滑动窗口（相对于 pglz 的 4 kB）可以在数据中找到更多反向引用，从而扩大压缩范围。 在类似的、完全不科学的工作负载中，LZ4 每 10,400 字节原始存储 111 字节（减少 98.9%），而 pglz 为 186 字节（减少 98.2%），并且使用了 312 kB 的总堆空间，而 pglz 为 472 kB。 在某些情况下，pglz 具有更好的压缩比。
  • 持续快速失败：LZ4 具有高效的早期中止机制，旨在快速检测随机或不可压缩数据。

Postgres 存储策略

首先，您需要知道 Postgres 对列有几种不同的存储策略：

EXTENDED（我们将其大写是因为 Postgres 文档这样做，而不是因为我们大喊大叫）让 Postgres 使用所有可用的工具。 这是 TEXT、VARCHAR、BYTEA 和 JSONB 等可变长度类型的默认设置。 值可以以未压缩、压缩的方式存储在堆中或 TOAST 中。

PLAIN 将列内联存储在堆中，未压缩。 它是 INT、FLOAT 和 BOOL 等固定宽度类型的默认值。 这些值很少从压缩中受益。

EXTERNAL 告诉 Postgres 将列存储在 TOAST 中，但不压缩它。

MAIN 告诉 Postgres 尝试压缩列，但如果可能的话避免将其移动到 TOAST。

瓦莱纳格式

对于可变长度类型，Postgres 使用一种称为 varlena 的格式来存储数据。 varlena 是一种自描述格式，其标头记录了数据的长度以及是否被压缩。 它用于所有可变长度类型（包括 TEXT、VARCHAR、BYTEA 和 JSONB），并且存储在堆、TOAST 和索引中。

仅压缩可变长度类型。 固定长度类型（如 INT、FLOAT 和 BOOL）永远不会被压缩；它们直接存储在堆中。

压缩决策树

当写入数据（插入或更新）时，Postgres 尝试使行大小低于大约 2 kB 的阈值（toast_tuple_target，默认 2040 字节）。 它对扩展列使用以下决策树：

  1. 如果整行已经小于约 2 kB，Postgres 会将该行直接写入堆，不进行压缩。
  2. 如果该行超过阈值，Postgres 将按大小对 EXTENDED 列进行排序，并尝试压缩最大的列。 如果压缩成功并使总行大小低于阈值，它将停止并写入堆。 如果不是，它将移动到下一个最大的列。
  3. 如果所有符合条件的列都被压缩并且该行仍然超过阈值，Postgres 开始将最大的 EXTENDED 或 EXTERNAL 列移出行外到 TOAST 表中，并用主堆元组中的 18 字节指针替换每个列，直到该行适合为止。
  4. 如果仍然不适合，Postgres 会循环返回以内联压缩所有 MAIN 列。 如果失败，则采取最后的手段：将那些 MAIN 列移至 TOAST 中。

有关 TOAST 的更多信息，请查看 Postgres TOAST：自切片面包以来最伟大的事情？

检查实际压缩节省

-- pg_column_size: bytes as stored in the heap or TOAST table (after compression)
-- octet_length:  bytes of raw character data (uncompressed)

SELECT
  pg_column_size(payload) AS stored_bytes,
  octet_length(payload)   AS raw_bytes,
  round(
    (1 - pg_column_size(payload)::numeric
          / NULLIF(octet_length(payload), 0)) * 100, 1
  ) AS compression_pct
FROM events WHERE length(payload) > 100 LIMIT 5;

pg_column_size 报告压缩数据大小。 当它返回一个远小于octet_length的值时，该值已成功压缩。 当两者大致相等时，该值压缩得不够好，无法节省空间。 在这种情况下，如果该值很大（高于 ~2 kB 阈值），它仍会以未压缩的方式移至 TOAST 表。

索引压缩

B 树索引页将键值存储为 IndexTuple 条目。 每个条目包含一个 IndexTupleData 标头（8 个字节），后跟关键数据。 对于可变长度类型，数据使用与堆元组相同的 varlena 格式（搭载为 8kB 行限制构建的解决方法）。

Postgres 使用与 TOAST 架构相关的单一压缩框架。 它在 varlena 标头中标记数据是否被压缩。 如果堆已经压缩了一个值，它将进入压缩索引。 如果某个值未压缩且超过 510 字节（TOAST_INDEX_TARGET，大约 8 kB 缓冲区页的 1/16），则索引代码会内联调用相同的 TOAST 压缩例程以尝试使其适合。 如果压缩的形式适合，则将其压缩存储。 如果压缩形式超过限制，写入事务就会失败。

这就是 Repeat('x', 5000) 可以被 B 树索引的原因：LZ4 将 5,000 个重复字符压缩到约 38 个字节，完全在 2704 字节的上限之内。 相同长度的随机或伪随机数据产生的压缩形式几乎等于原始数据，超出了上限并且无法索引。

-- These succeed: both compressible, both fit after LZ4 compression
CREATE INDEX ON docs (body);

INSERT INTO docs VALUES (repeat('x', 5000));    -- stored: ~38 bytes compressed
INSERT INTO docs VALUES (repeat('ab', 2000));   -- stored: ~35 bytes compressed

-- This fails: md5 output is pseudo-random, essentially incompressible
INSERT INTO docs VALUES (
  (SELECT string_agg(md5(g::text), '') FROM generate_series(1, 88) g)
);
-- 2816 chars of MD5 → compressed form ≈ 2816 bytes → index row size 2832 > 2704
-- ERROR:  index row size 2832 exceeds btree version 4 maximum 2704 for index "..."
-- HINT:   Values larger than 1/3 of a buffer page cannot be indexed.

Postgres 压缩的未来

关于 Postgres 如何通过压缩来向前发展，有很多东西需要了解。 专用压缩数据类型的早期错误步骤得到了承认，并且底层 pglz 工作被重新整合到 TOAST 中，这取得了巨大的成功。 在从 pglz 迁移到 LZ4 时，Postgres 采取了类似的方法：首先进行测试，然后进行迁移。 核心团队有意采取行动，以确保压缩算法的更改是正确的路径。
